meta_title: For Founders: passive income to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Passive Income to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to generate passive income and overcome the 'no project ideas' trap. An intermediate-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Passive income is built through functional micro-SaaS utilities that solve high-friction, low-complexity problems. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative development, you can deploy value-driven tools in hours, testing market demand before committing to long-term development cycles.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the silent killer of ambition. You stare at your editor, paralyzed by the phantom fear that your next project might be a waste of time. You aren't just lacking an idea; you are lacking a mechanism to validate market demand without sacrificing months of your life to code that nobody wants to buy.

True passive income for developers isn't about viral hits; it's about building 'boring' software that solves recurring business pain. By shifting your focus from 'innovation' to 'utility,' you can build, deploy, and monetize micro-SaaS tools that generate revenue while you sleep. This is the blueprint for turning technical capability into a sustainable, automated income stream.

steps.title: Identify High-Velocity Micro-Problems
steps.description: Don't reinvent the wheel. Scan marketplaces like G2 or Reddit for 'how do I automate X' queries. Focus on tasks that take 10 minutes manually but are done daily.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the following list of manual business tasks and identify three that can be solved with a single-purpose micro-SaaS tool. Rank them by ease of implementation and monetization potential.
steps.claude_code_command: claude research 'current trends in B2B micro-SaaS automation'

steps.title: Scaffold the MVP Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a clean, production-ready boilerplate. Speed to market is your only true competitive advantage at this stage.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Next.js 14 boilerplate with Tailwind CSS, Supabase authentication, and Stripe integration for a subscription-based micro-SaaS.

steps.title: Implement the Core Value Proposition
steps.description: Focus exclusively on the one feature that provides the primary benefit. Remove all 'nice-to-have' bloat to ensure the code is maintainable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean, modular service layer in TypeScript to handle the core automation logic for my tool. Ensure it follows SOLID principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'implement the core business logic based on the service layer specs'

steps.title: Automate Data Persistence & Stripe
steps.description: Connect your logic to a database and payment gateway. This is the bridge between a project and a business.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure API route in Next.js to handle Stripe webhooks and update user subscription status in the Supabase database.

steps.title: Deploy and Monitor for Feedback
steps.description: Push to production immediately. Use telemetry to see how users interact with your tool, not how you think they should.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a monitoring dashboard configuration to track user conversion rates from free trial to paid subscription.

pro_tips: Never build an auth system from scratch; use Clerk or Supabase Auth to save 20 hours of dev time.
Focus on 'pain-killer' SaaS, not 'vitamin' SaaS; solve problems that cost users money, not just time.
Set a strict 48-hour deadline for your MVP; if it takes longer, you are over-engineering.
Always include a 'Request a Feature' button in your MVP to capture user-led roadmap ideas.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests; high test coverage is the best way to ensure passive income remains truly passive.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, spend 80% of your time on distribution. Reach out to the communities where you found the initial problem and offer free access in exchange for honest feedback and testimonials.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting great ideas die in your head because you're waiting for the 'right' time. The best time to build was yesterday; the second best time is now. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
